docs.title: Create an APT repository on CentOS
docs.text: Systems like Ubuntu, Debian or Mint use apt packages. You can also share apt packages from CentOS by creating an apt repository stored locally with GPG-signed packages.

docs.title: Preparing gpg keys
docs.text:
Generating new keys can require some time for entropy generation. as root, no sudo!

gpg --gen-key

On the first run, break after it has generated directories and files in ~/.gnupg/. Then add the SHA256 requirement to gpg.conf:

cat <<'EOF' >> ~/.gnupg/gpg.conf
cert-digest-algo SHA256
digest-algo SHA256
EOF

Run gpg again and go through all the prompts to generate the key:

gpg --gen-key

This step might take a long time. To speed up entropy generation, run in another terminal: 

while true; do dd if=/dev/sda of=/dev/zero; find / | xargs file >/dev/null 2>&1; done

Stop it with ctrl+c when gpg key generation finishes. Then export the generated keys:

gpg --list-keys

gpg --output your-PUBLIC-key-name-here.gpg --armor --export 123456AB

gpg --output your-PRIVATE-key-name-here.gpg --armor --export-secret-key 123456AB

Place the public key in the repository directory.

docs.title: Installing required packages
docs.text: yum -y install epel-release dpkg-dev tar

docs.title: Script to build the repository
docs.text:
updatescript=/mnt/mirror/ubuntu/example-ubuntu/update-repo.sh
cat <<'EOFSH' >${updatescript}
#!/bin/sh

# working directory
repodir=/mnt/mirror/ubuntu/example-ubuntu/
cd ${repodir}

# create the package index
dpkg-scanpackages -m . > Packages
cat Packages | gzip -9c > Packages.gz

# create the Release file
PKGS=$(wc -c Packages)
PKGS_GZ=$(wc -c Packages.gz)
cat <<EOF > Release
Architectures: all
Date: \$(date -R)
MD5Sum:
 $(md5sum Packages  | cut -d" " -f1) $PKGS
 $(md5sum Packages.gz  | cut -d" " -f1) $PKGS_GZ
SHA1:
 $(sha1sum Packages  | cut -d" " -f1) $PKGS
 $(sha1sum Packages.gz  | cut -d" " -f1) $PKGS_GZ
SHA256:
 $(sha256sum Packages | cut -d" " -f1) $PKGS
 $(sha256sum Packages.gz | cut -d" " -f1) $PKGS_GZ
EOF
gpg -abs -o Release.gpg Release
EOFSH
chmod 755 ${updatescript}

docs.title: Adding packages to the repository
docs.text:
Move any package to the repository directory and run the update script:

./update-repo.sh

To use the repository as a client, import the public key and add an entry in sources.list:

sudo wget http://mirror.example.com/ubuntu/your-PUBLIC-key-name-here.gpg

sudo apt-key add /root/your-PUBLIC-key-name-here.gpg

sudo wget http://mirror.example.com/ubuntu/example-ubuntu/example-ubuntu.list -O /etc/apt/sources.list.d/example-ubuntu.list

apt-get update

docs.title: How to bring the port up from the flapping state on Cisco?
docs.text:
Locate the flapping port:

sh int status

Enter configuration mode and select the port (using eth1/6 as an example):

conf t

int eth1/6

Bounce the port:

shut

no shut

Exit configuration mode and save:

exit

exit

wr

docs.title: How To Copy a MySQL Database
docs.text:
Copy a MySQL database on the same server or between servers. This also works for copying individual tables.

In this example, db_test is copied to db_test_2. db_test must already exist.

Create the target database:

mysql -u root -p

CREATE DATABASE db_test_2;

docs.title: a) If you want to move the whole database
docs.text: mysqldump -u root -p db_test > /tmp/db_test.sql

docs.title: b) If you want to move only certain tables
docs.text:
mysqldump -u root -p db_test tablename1 tablename2 > /tmp/db_test_tables.sql

Import the dump into the new database:

mysql -u root -p db_test_2 < db_test_tables.sql

docs.title: Delete git branch
docs.text:
Syntax:

git branch (-d | -D) [-r] <branchname>...

Delete a local branch:

git branch -d dev

If the branch has unmerged changes, git will refuse:

error: The branch `dev` is not fully merged.
If you are sure you want to delete it, run `git branch -D dev`.

Force-delete a local branch with unmerged changes:

git branch -D dev

Delete a remote branch:

git push -d origin dev

To github.com:obol89/test.git
- [deleted] dev

docs.title: Creating empty file to take disk space on Linux
docs.text:
Useful for testing disk alerting or verifying filesystem behavior.

dd if=/dev/zero of=/var/filename2 bs=$((1024*1024)) count=$((16*1024))

count is a multiple of 1024 MB.

Alternative:

fallocate -l 85G filename3

docs.title: How to extend LVM in Linux
Physical Volumes
docs.text:
pvs

PV         VG             Fmt  Attr PSize   PFree
/dev/sda2  centos_centos7 lvm2 a--  <19.00g    0

docs.title: Volume Groups
docs.text:
vgs

VG             #PV #LV #SN Attr   VSize   VFree
centos_centos7   1   2   0 wz--n- <19.00g    0

docs.title: Logical Volumes
docs.text:
lvs

LV   VG             Attr       LSize   Pool Origin Data%  Meta%  Move Log Cpy%Sync Convert
root centos_centos7 -wi-ao---- <17.00g
swap centos_centos7 -wi-ao----   2.00g

Locate the new volume to add as a Physical Volume:

fdisk -l

Disk /dev/sda: 21.5 GB, 21474836480 bytes, 41943040 sectors
Units = sectors of 1 * 512 = 512 bytes
Sector size (logical/physical): 512 bytes / 512 bytes
I/O size (minimum/optimal): 512 bytes / 512 bytes
Disk label type: dos
Disk identifier: 0x000b3a0f

   Device Boot      Start         End      Blocks   Id  System
/dev/sda1   *        2048     2099199     1048576   83  Linux
/dev/sda2         2099200    41943039    19921920   8e  Linux LVM

Disk /dev/sdb: 10.7 GB, 10737418240 bytes, 20971520 sectors
Units = sectors of 1 * 512 = 512 bytes
Sector size (logical/physical): 512 bytes / 512 bytes
I/O size (minimum/optimal): 512 bytes / 512 bytes


Disk /dev/mapper/centos_centos7-root: 18.2 GB, 18249416704 bytes, 35643392 sectors
Units = sectors of 1 * 512 = 512 bytes
Sector size (logical/physical): 512 bytes / 512 bytes
I/O size (minimum/optimal): 512 bytes / 512 bytes


Disk /dev/mapper/centos_centos7-swap: 2147 MB, 2147483648 bytes, 4194304 sectors
Units = sectors of 1 * 512 = 512 bytes
Sector size (logical/physical): 512 bytes / 512 bytes
I/O size (minimum/optimal): 512 bytes / 512 bytes

Partition the new disk:

fdisk /dev/sdb

To create new partition Press n Choose primary partition user p Change the type using t Type 8e to change the partition type to Linux LVM Use p to print the create partition Press w to write the changes 

Welcome to fdisk (util-linux 2.23.2).

Changes will remain in memory only, until you decide to write them.
Be careful before using the write command.

Device does not contain a recognized partition table
Building a new DOS disklabel with disk identifier 0x38e14a7a.

Command (m for help): n
Partition type:
   p   primary (0 primary, 0 extended, 4 free)
   e   extended
Select (default p): p
Partition number (1-4, default 1):
First sector (2048-20971519, default 2048):
Using default value 2048
Last sector, +sectors or +size{K,M,G} (2048-20971519, default 20971519):
Using default value 20971519
Partition 1 of type Linux and of size 10 GiB is set

Command (m for help): t
Selected partition 1
Hex code (type L to list all codes): 8e

The partition table has been altered!

Calling ioctl() to re-read partition table.

Create the new Physical Volume:

pvcreate /dev/sdb1

Physical volume /dev/sdb1 successfully created.

Verify:

pvs

PV         VG             Fmt  Attr PSize   PFree
/dev/sda2  centos_centos7 lvm2 a--  <19.00g      0
/dev/sdb1                 lvm2 ---  <10.00g <10.00g

Add the PV to the volume group:

vgextend centos_centos7 /dev/sdb1

Volume group "centos_centos7" successfully extended

VG             #PV #LV #SN Attr   VSize  VFree
centos_centos7   2   2   0 wz--n- 28.99g <10.00g

lvextend -l +100%FREE /dev/centos_centos7/root

Logical volume centos_centos7/root successfully resized.

xfs_growfs /dev/centos_centos7/root

docs.title: Extending disk space on Linux without LVM
docs.text: This method requires host rebooting and using lsblk, fdisk tools. Removing or creating a partition doesn't remove a data or a filesystem on a disk. It will only update the MBR or GPT information where partition lies and its size.

docs.title: Find partition to extend
docs.text:
lsblk

NAME   MAJ:MIN RM  SIZE RO TYPE MOUNTPOINT
sda      8:0    0   20G  0 disk
├─sda1   8:1    0  4.1G  0 part /
├─sda2   8:2    0    1K  0 part
├─sda5   8:5    0  1.7G  0 part /var
├─sda6   8:6    0  976M  0 part [SWAP]
├─sda7   8:7    0  371M  0 part /tmp
└─sda8   8:8    0 12.9G  0 part /home
sr0     11:0    1 1024M  0 rom

Let's say we would like to extend /var partition

docs.title: Add diskspace, extend disk, i.e. in vCenter, VirtualBox
docs.text: Wherever you have your virtual machine installed, you need to shut it down and increase disk size.

docs.title: Use fdisk to delete and re-add partition
docs.text:
fdisk /dev/sda

Welcome to fdisk (util-linux 2.36.1).
Changes will remain in memory only, until you decide to write them.
Be careful before using the write command.

docs.title: Print the partition table
docs.text:
Command (m for help): p
Disk /dev/sda: 20 GiB, 21474836480 bytes, 41943040 sectors
Disk model: VBOX HARDDISK
Units: sectors of 1 * 512 = 512 bytes
Sector size (logical/physical): 512 bytes / 512 bytes
I/O size (minimum/optimal): 512 bytes / 512 bytes
Disklabel type: dos
Disk identifier: 0xfeb22017

Device     Boot    Start      End  Sectors  Size Id Type
/dev/sda1  *        2048  8638463  8636416  4.1G 83 Linux
/dev/sda2        8640510 41940991 33300482 15.9G  5 Extended
/dev/sda5        8640512 12167167  3526656  1.7G 83 Linux
/dev/sda6       12169216 14168063  1998848  976M 82 Linux swap / Solaris
/dev/sda7       14170112 14929919   759808  371M 83 Linux
/dev/sda8       14931968 41940991 27009024 12.9G 83 Linux

docs.title: Delete the partition we want to extend with d command
docs.text:
Command (m for help): d
Partition number (1,2,5-8, default 8): 5

Partition 5 has been deleted.

docs.title: Create a new partition with n command
docs.text:
Be careful with which blocks you select here.

Command (m for help): n
All space for primary partitions is in use.
Adding logical partition 8
First sector (8642558-12167167, default 8642560):
Last sector, +/-sectors or +/-size{K,M,G,T,P} (8642560-12167167, default 12167167):

Created a new partition 8 of type 'Linux' and of size 1.7 GiB.

docs.title: Save new partition table with w command
docs.text:
Command (m for help): w
The partition table has been altered.

The kernel still uses the old partitions. The new table will be used at the next reboot.
Syncing disks.

docs.title: Resize filesystem for new partition
docs.text:
resize2fs /dev/sda8

resize2fs 1.46.2 (28-Feb-2021)

docs.title: Delete files older than X days
docs.text:
List files older than 2 days:

find /home/user/some-folders-*/var/ -type f -name "*.log" -mtime +2

After confirming, delete them:

find /home/trades/venus-AvkLinkServer-*/var/ -type f -name "*.log" -mtime +2 -exec rm {} \;

docs.title: Gather or update ansible facts
docs.text:
Ansible facts are data related to your remote systems, accessible via the ansible_facts variable.

Gather or update facts from a specific server (the -e parameter for a custom Python interpreter is optional):

ansible -i /etc/ansible/hosts yourserver.yourdomain.com -m gather_facts -e 'ansible_python_interpreter=/usr/bin/python'

docs.title: Host is not allowed to connect to this MySQL server
Symptoms
docs.text:
Error: The test connection to the database server has failed because of network problems:

Host '203.0.113.2' is not allowed to connect to this MySQL server

docs.title: Cause
docs.text: root user is not allowed to access the MySQL database server remotely.

docs.title: Solution
docs.text:
Log in to MySQL on the remote server and create a user allowed to connect from 203.0.113.2:

mysql> CREATE USER 'root'@'203.0.113.2' IDENTIFIED BY 'root_password';

mysql> GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON *.* TO 'root'@'203.0.113.2' WITH GRANT OPTION;

mysql> FLUSH PRIVILEGES;

Or allow access from any host:

mysql> CREATE USER 'root'@'%' IDENTIFIED BY 'root_password';

mysql> GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON *.* TO 'root'@'%' WITH GRANT OPTION;

mysql> FLUSH PRIVILEGES;

docs.title: Increasing Swap Space Using a Swap File on Linux
Step 1: Turn Off the Existing Swap
docs.text: sudo swapoff -a

docs.title: Step 2: Create a New Swap File
docs.text:
Create an 8GB swap file:

sudo dd if=/dev/zero of=/swapfile bs=1G count=8

Adjust the count value to change the size of the swap file.

docs.title: Step 3: Set the Correct Permissions
docs.text: sudo chmod 0600 /swapfile

docs.title: Step 4: Set Up the Swap Area
docs.text: sudo mkswap /swapfile

docs.title: Step 5: Enable the Swap File
docs.text: sudo swapon /swapfile

docs.title: Step 6: Make the Swap File Permanent
docs.text:
Add to /etc/fstab:

/swapfile none swap defaults 0 0

docs.title: How To Install Percona MySQL Server 8 on CentOS 8 / RHEL 8
docs.text:
Update the system first:

sudo yum -y update

docs.title: Add Percona YUM repository
docs.text: sudo yum install https://repo.percona.com/yum/percona-release-latest.noarch.rpm

docs.title: Enable repository for MySQL 8.0
docs.text:
sudo percona-release setup ps80

When prompted, allow disabling the RHEL 8 MySQL module.

docs.title: Install Percona Server for MySQL 8.0
docs.text:
sudo yum install percona-server-server percona-toolkit

sudo percona-release enable-only tools release

sudo yum install percona-xtrabackup-80

docs.title: Start and secure Percona MySQL Server
docs.text:
sudo systemctl enable mysqld

sudo systemctl start mysqld

docs.title: Find generated root temporary password
docs.text:
sudo grep "temporary password" /var/log/mysqld.log

Then run the secure installation:

mysql_secure_installation

docs.title: Show iptables with line numbers
docs.text:
iptables -nL --line-numbers

-n – Numeric output. IP addresses and port numbers will be printed in numeric format. -L – List all rules in the selected chain. If no chain is selected, all chains are listed.

docs.title: Insert (add) rule in the specific place in iptables as it works descending
docs.text:
sudo iptables -I INPUT 9 -i eth0 -d 192.168.1.254 -j ACCEPT -m comment --comment "my new comment"

The number 9 means the rule is inserted at position 9 in the chain.

docs.title: Clone and reset KVM virtual machine
Clone your VM and spawn new instances in KVM
docs.text:
List all VMs:

virsh list --all

If the target VM is running, suspend it:

virsh suspend debian12-base

Clone the VM:

virt-clone --original debian12-base --name debian12-base-clone01 --auto-clone

Resume the suspended base:

virsh resume debian12-base

docs.title: Prepare new system to be fresh and working
docs.text: virt-sysprep -d debian12-base-clone01  --root-password password:abcABC123 --firstboot-command 'dpkg-reconfigure openssh-server'

docs.title: Update Linux firmware from terminal
docs.text:
Requires fwupd (install via package manager if missing).

Update your system first:

sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y

Start the daemon, refresh available firmware, and install updates:

sudo systemctl start fwupd

sudo fwupdmgr refresh

sudo fwupdmgr update

docs.title: MySQL 8 – Authentication plugin ‘caching_sha2_password’ cannot be loaded
docs.text:
MySQL 8 changed the default authentication plugin to caching_sha2_password, which older clients do not support. Switch the user to native password authentication:

ALTER USER ‘username’@’ip_address’ IDENTIFIED WITH mysql_native_password BY ‘password’;

docs.title: How to change MySQL Password Policy Level
docs.text:
MySQL servers ship with a validate password plugin that enforces a password policy. This can cause the error: ERROR 1819 (HY000): Your password does not satisfy the current policy requirements.

The policy level can be changed at runtime via the MySQL CLI or permanently in the config file (my.cnf or mysqld.cnf).

View current validate_password settings:

mysql> SHOW VARIABLES LIKE ‘validate_password%’;

+--------------------------------------+--------+
| Variable_name                        | Value  |
+--------------------------------------+--------+
| validate_password.check_user_name    | OFF    |
| validate_password.dictionary_file    |        |
| validate_password.length             | 8      |
| validate_password.mixed_case_count   | 1      |
| validate_password.number_count       | 1      |
| validate_password.policy             | MEDIUM |
| validate_password.special_char_count | 1      |
+--------------------------------------+--------+
7 rows in set (0.06 sec)

The default level is MEDIUM. Change it to LOW (requires only minimum 8-character length):

mysql> SET GLOBAL validate_password.policy=LOW;

Query OK, 0 rows affected (0.02 sec)

To make this permanent, add to the MySQL configuration (my.cnf):

[mysqld]
validate_password.policy=LOW

docs.title: How to get the size of a table in MySQL
docs.text:
The INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES table contains around 20 columns. To determine disk space used by tables, we need two columns: DATA_LENGTH and INDEX_LENGTH.

  • DATA_LENGTH is the length (or size) of all data in the table (in bytes).
  • INDEX_LENGTH is the length (or size) of the index file for the table (also in bytes).

Query to list all tables in a database with their size in megabytes:

SELECT
  TABLE_NAME AS `Table`,
  ROUND((DATA_LENGTH + INDEX_LENGTH) / 1024 / 1024) AS `Size (MB)`
FROM
  information_schema.TABLES
WHERE
  TABLE_SCHEMA = "bookstore"
ORDER BY
  (DATA_LENGTH + INDEX_LENGTH)
DESC;

Single-line version:

SELECT TABLE_NAME AS `Table`, ROUND((DATA_LENGTH + INDEX_LENGTH) /
 1024 / 1024) AS `Size (MB)` FROM information_schema.TABLES WHERE
TABLE_SCHEMA = "bookstore" ORDER BY (DATA_LENGTH + INDEX_LENGTH) DESC;

Example output:

+----------------------------------+-----------+
| Table                            | Size (MB) |
+----------------------------------+-----------+
| book                             |       267 |
| author                           |        39 |
| post                             |        27 |
| cache                            |        24 |
...

To get the size of a single table, add AND TABLE_NAME = “your_table_name” to the WHERE clause:

SELECT
  TABLE_NAME AS `Table`,
  ROUND((DATA_LENGTH + INDEX_LENGTH) / 1024 / 1024) AS `Size (MB)`
FROM
  information_schema.TABLES
WHERE
    TABLE_SCHEMA = "bookstore"
  AND
    TABLE_NAME = "book"
ORDER BY
  (DATA_LENGTH + INDEX_LENGTH)
DESC;

Single-line version:

SELECT TABLE_NAME AS `Table`, ROUND((DATA_LENGTH + INDEX_LENGTH) /
 1024 / 1024) AS `Size (MB)` FROM information_schema.TABLES WHERE
TABLE_SCHEMA = "bookstore" AND TABLE_NAME = "book" ORDER BY (DATA_LENGTH
 + INDEX_LENGTH) DESC;

+-------+-----------+
| Table | Size (MB) |
+-------+-----------+
| book  |       267 |
+-------+-----------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)

docs.title: Create partman LVM partitions on Debian with preseed
docs.text:
Preseed template for Debian LVM partitions with partman. The boot partition must be created first, then the volume group, then individual logical volumes assigned to that group.

d-i partman-auto/expert_recipe string                 \
      root ::                                         \
            300 512 2048 ext4                         \
                method{ format } format{ }            \
                $primary{ } $bootable{ }              \
                use_filesystem{ } filesystem{ ext4 }  \
                mountpoint{ /boot }                   \
            .                                         \
            2048 20480 -1 xfs                         \
                method{ lvm }                         \
                device{ /dev/sda }                    \
                vg_name{ vg00 }                       \
            .                                         \
            512 1024 2048 linux-swap                  \
                method{ swap } format{ }              \
                $lvmok{ }                             \
                in_vg{ vg00 }                         \
                lv_name{ swap }                       \
            .                                         \
            10240 20480 20480 xfs                     \
                method{ format } format{ }            \
                $lvmok{ }                             \
                lv_name{ root }                       \
                in_vg{ vg00 }                         \
                use_filesystem{ } filesystem{ xfs }   \
                mountpoint{ / }                       \
            .                                         \
            10240 20480 20480 xfs                     \
                method{ format } format{ }            \
                $lvmok{ }                             \
                lv_name{ var }                        \
                in_vg{ vg00 }                         \
                use_filesystem{ } filesystem{ xfs }   \
                mountpoint{ /var }                    \
            .                                         \
            10240 20480 20480 xfs                     \
                method{ format } format{ }            \
                $lvmok{ }                             \
                lv_name{ opt }                        \
                in_vg{ vg00 }                         \
                use_filesystem{ } filesystem{ xfs }   \
                mountpoint{ /opt }                    \
            .                                         \
            10240 20480 -1 xfs                        \
                method{ format } format{ }            \
                $lvmok{ }                             \
                lv_name{ home }                       \
                in_vg{ vg00 }                         \
                use_filesystem{ } filesystem{ xfs }   \
                mountpoint{ /home }                   \
            .                                         \

docs.title: How to repair corrupted USB drive in Linux
docs.text:
If your USB drive is unreadable, unwritable, or showing a reduced size, follow the steps below.

Locate the USB drive name with lsblk.

docs.title: Remove old partitions
docs.text:
Open fdisk and delete all existing partitions:

fdisk /dev/sdc

Type d and press enter and proceed with d and enter to delete another portion, if necessary.

docs.title: New partitions
docs.text:
Next, we need to create a new partition. Type n for new partition. Type p to make this primary. Type 1 to make this the first partition. enter to accept default first sector and last sector. Type w to write the new partition information

Unmount the drive:

umount /dev/sdc1

docs.title: New filesystem
docs.text:
mkfs.ext4 -f /dev/sdc1

or

mkfs.ntfs -f /dev/sdc1

docs.title: How to repair corrupted USB drive in Windows
docs.text:
Restore a USB drive using Diskpart.

Open a Command Prompt as administrator (cmd.exe) and run:

  1. Diskpart
  2. List Disk
  3. Select Disk X (where X is the disk number of your USB drive)
  4. Clean
  5. Create Partition Primary
  6. Format fs=Fat32 Quick (you can also use NTFS or exFAT)
  7. Active

docs.title: How to use SCP command to transfer files
docs.text:
SCP (secure copy) is a command-line utility that allows you to securely copy files and directories between two locations.

Syntax:

scp [-346BCpqrTv] [-c cipher] [-F ssh_config] [-i identity_file] [-J destination] [-l limit] [-o ssh_option] [-P port] [-S program] source ... target

All options are documented in man scp.

docs.title: Copy files and directories between two systems
docs.text:
scp file.txt username@ip_address:/remote/directory

If SSH on the remote host is listening on a non-default port, specify it with -P:

scp -P 2232 file.txt username@ip_address:/remote/directory

To copy a directory recursively, use -r:

scp -r /local/directory username@ip_address:/remote/directory

docs.title: How to use basic screen commands on Linux
docs.text:
List screen sessions:

screen -ls

Name and open new screen session:

screen -S session_name

Reattach screen session:

screen -r sessions_name

docs.title: New split
docs.text:
• Ctrl + a then | : vertical split.
  • Ctrl + a then S : horizontal split.

docs.title: Close split
docs.text:
• Ctrl + a then X : closes active split.
  • Ctrl + a then Q : closes all splits.

docs.title: Move around splits
docs.text:
• Ctrl + a then n : navigate between window sessions (lowest to highest index).
  • Ctrl + a then Tab : move input cursor to the next split area.

docs.title: New command
docs.text: • Ctrl + a then c : create a new window within the current screen instance.

docs.title: Other
docs.text:
• Ctrl + a then d : detach from the session and return to the original shell. Screen continues running in the background.
  • Ctrl + a then x : lock the screen session with a password.

docs.title: Update Snap package and snap-store
docs.text:
Tested on Ubuntu 22.04. Kills the current snap-store process and upgrades all snap packages.

sudo killall snap-store && snap refresh

docs.title: How to test link speed between two hosts
docs.text: Uses iperf.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
On both hosts:

sudo apt-get install iperf

docs.title: Start iperf as a server on one of the machines
docs.text: iperf -s

docs.title: Run iperf as a client
docs.text:
iperf -c IPERF_SERVER_ADDRESS

Example output:

------------------------------------------------------------
Client connecting to IPERF_SERVER_ADDRESS, TCP port 5001
TCP window size: 16.0 KByte (default)
------------------------------------------------------------
[  3] local 192.168.0.4 port 37248 connected with 192.168.0.5 port 5001
[ ID] Interval       Transfer     Bandwidth
[  3]  0.0-10.0 sec  1.04 GBytes    893 Mbits/sec

The default port is 5001. Use -p to change it.

docs.title: Required permissions for SSH keys, files and folders
docs.text: Item Sample Numeric Bitwise Command SSH folder ~/.ssh 700 drwx------ chmod 0700 ~/.ssh Public key ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub 644 -rw-r--r-- chmod 0644 ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub Private key ~/.ssh/id_rsa 600 -rw------- chmod 0600 ~/.ssh/id_rsa Home folder ~ 755 at most drwxr-xr-x at most chmod 0755 ~

docs.title: Configuration of a static IP address on CentOS 8
docs.text:
This covers configuring a static IP by editing config files directly.

Identify the interface to configure:

ip a

1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
2: enp0s3: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc fq_codel state UP group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 08:00:27:d7:61:91 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 192.168.178.89/24 brd 192.168.178.255 scope global dynamic noprefixroute enp0s3
       valid_lft 85177sec preferred_lft 85177sec
    inet6 2002:c2bf:e06a:0:9204:bf9d:ab95:aa91/128 scope global dynamic noprefixroute
       valid_lft 5978sec preferred_lft 2378sec
    inet6 2002:c2bf:e06a:0:771:5fa8:a2c7:4322/64 scope global dynamic noprefixroute
       valid_lft 6956sec preferred_lft 3356sec
    inet6 fe80::9204:bf9d:ab95:aa91/64 scope link noprefixroute
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
3: virbr0: <NO-CARRIER,BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state DOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 52:54:00:13:f3:a7 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 192.168.122.1/24 brd 192.168.122.255 scope global virbr0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
4: virbr0-nic: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST> mtu 1500 qdisc fq_codel master virbr0 state DOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 52:54:00:13:f3:a7 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff

Edit or create the config file for this interface:

vim /etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ifcfg-ens10

TYPE=Ethernet
PROXY_METHOD=none
BROWSER_ONLY=no
BOOTPROTO=static
DEFROUTE=yes
IPV4_FAILURE_FATAL=no
IPV6INIT=yes
IPV6_AUTOCONF=yes
IPV6_DEFROUTE=yes
IPV6_FAILURE_FATAL=no
IPV6_ADDR_GEN_MODE=stable-privacy
NAME=enp0s3
UUID=6f23bb02-a7bf-412d-beca-4b425d866596
DEVICE=enp0s3
ONBOOT=yes
IPADDR=192.168.178.89
PREFIX=24
GATEWAY=192.168.178.1

Parameters like IPADDR, PREFIX, GATEWAY, DNS1, DNS2 are essential.

Set BOOTPROTO to none or static.

docs.title: Two default gateways on CentOS
docs.text:
You have two or more network cards (interfaces) in one Linux system and each of these interfaces has its own default gateway. By default, you can only have one default gateway on a system.

We assume that we have two interfaces: eth0 eth1

Two networks that should be used are: 192.168.1.0/24 10.10.0.0/24

whereby the first IP address in each respective network should be the gateway. Config files for these interfaces are in /etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/.

/etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ifcfg-eth0:

TYPE=Ethernet
PROXY_METHOD=none
BROWSER_ONLY=no
BOOTPROTO=none
DEFROUTE=yes
IPV4_FAILURE_FATAL=no
IPV6INIT=no
IPV6_AUTOCONF="yes"
IPV6_DEFROUTE="yes"
IPV6_FAILURE_FATAL="no"
IPV6_ADDR_GEN_MODE="stable-privacy"
NAME=eth0
UUID=7f164bfd-1ae4-4062-aadd-e2ea8bc0121e
DEVICE=eth0
ONBOOT=yes
IPADDR=192.168.1.5
PREFIX=24
GATEWAY=192.168.1.1
DNS1=8.8.8.8
DNS2=1.1.1.1

/etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ifcfg-eth1:

TYPE=Ethernet
PROXY_METHOD=none
BROWSER_ONLY=no
BOOTPROTO=none
IPADDR=10.10.0.124
PREFIX=24
DNS1=8.8.8.8
DNS2=1.1.1.1
DEFROUTE=yes
IPV4_FAILURE_FATAL=no
IPV6INIT=no
NAME=eth1
UUID=9320b706-e68e-3610-9fec-9988316bd478
DEVICE=eth1
ONBOOT=yes
GATEWAY=10.10.0.1
NETMASK=255.255.255.0
NM_CONTROLLED=yes

To use a second interface and address, add another routing table. In /etc/iproute2/rt_tables, add 1 rt2 at the end:

#
# reserved values
#
255     local
254     main
253     default
0       unspec
#
# local
#
#1      inr.ruhep
1 rt2

Add routing rules and routes:

ip route add default via 10.10.0.1 dev eth1 table rt2
ip rule add from 10.10.0.0/24 table rt2

Verify the changes:

ip route show table rt2
ip rule show

To persist these changes across reboots, create a systemd service. First, create the script /usr/local/bin/default_routes.sh:

# !/bin/bash
ip route add default via 10.70.70.2 dev eth1 table int

Make it executable:

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/default_routes.sh

Create the systemd service file /etc/systemd/system/default_route.service:

[Unit]
Description=It adds default route for eth1
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/default_routes.sh
TimeoutStartSec=0

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

docs.title: How to move /var to new partition in CentOS 7
docs.text:
mkdir /mnt/var_new

mount /dev/vg00/lvo10 /mnt/var_new

rsync -avH /var/ /mnt/var_new

diff -r /var /mnt/var_new

mv /var /var_old

mkdir /var

Add fstab entry:

/dev/vg00/lvo10 /var xfs defaults 0 0

umount /mnt/var_new

mount /var
